content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Obsoletes `regulation of cytogamy` (GO:7770078) per curator feedback on #32085. The term was created last week as a grouping parent for `positive regulation of cytogamy` (GO:7770079), but GO does not auto-generate regulation triads and the grouping term has no curation use case.

## Changes

- `GO:7770078` is now `obsolete regulation of cytogamy`
- Removed `intersection_of` axioms
- Added `is_obsolete: true`, prefixed name and definition with "obsolete"/"OBSOLETE."
- Added comment explaining the obsoletion reason
- No `replaced_by` or `consider` — there is no replacement; the term simply isn't needed

`positive regulation of cytogamy` (GO:7770079) is untouched. Its logical definition (`positively_regulates some cytogamy`) and `part_of` link to `positive regulation of conjugation with cellular fusion` keep it correctly classified without the grouping parent.

## Impact

- No annotations exist (term was created 2026-05-22, never released to AmiGO)
- `obo-grep.pl 'GO:7770078' src/ontology/go-edit.obo` confirms no other term references it
- ELK reasoning succeeds; all SPARQL QC checks pass

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION — ontology validated before changes
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A (obsoletion of just-created term)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH — confirmed no internal references
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — N/A
- [x] EDITS — used obo-checkout / obo-checkin
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS — all axioms stripped from obsoleted term
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS — /term-obsoletion skill followed
- [x] METADATA — name, def, comment, is_obsolete, term_tracker_item all set per convention
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — SPARQL QC passes; ELK reasoning succeeds
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — N/A (no new references)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

---

- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood

#### Removed

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27798845" 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "regulation of cytogamy" 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) EquivalentTo [biological regulation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0065007) and ([regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) some [cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0000755))

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) SubClassOf [regulation of reproductive process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_2000241) 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) SubClassOf [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) some [cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0000755) 

#### Added
- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "OBSOLETE. Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of cytogamy." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27798845" 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) [deprecated](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#deprecated) true 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "This term was created as a grouping parent for positive regulation of cytogamy (GO:7770079), but the GO does not automatically generate regulation triads. The parent term is unnecessary as there is no curation use case." 

- [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "obsolete regulation of cytogamy" 


### positive regulation of cytogamy `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770079`
#### Removed
- [positive regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770079) SubClassOf [obsolete regulation of cytogamy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770078)

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0000755
name: cytogamy
namespace: biological_process
alt_id: GO:0000218
alt_id: GO:0030462
def: "A reproductive process in a single-celled organism in which the cytoplasm of two mating cells fuse, resulting in the formation of a single cell containing the combined cellular contents." [GOC:vw]
comment: Cytogamy follows cell recognition and mating projection formation (when present) and precedes or accompanies nuclear fusion (karyogamy).
synonym: "zygote formation" RELATED []
is_a: GO:0022413 ! reproductive process in single-celled organism
is_a: GO:0140253 ! cell-cell fusion
relationship: part_of GO:0000747 ! conjugation with cellular fusion
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31270" xsd:anyURI

The two inferred `is_a` parents come from cytogamy's own classification: `cytogamy` is a `reproductive process in single-celled organism` (→ `reproductive process`) and a `cell-cell fusion` (→ `cellular process`), so the reasoner places `positive regulation of cytogamy` under the corresponding `positive regulation of …` parents. The `part_of` link to `positive regulation of conjugation with cellular fusion` reflects that cytogamy is `part_of` `conjugation with cellular fusion`.

For comparison, on **current master** (before this PR), GO:7770079 also has `is_a: GO:7770078 regulation of cytogamy` — that link is what gets removed when GO:7770078 is obsoleted here. No other parents are lost.

content.text:
Posted the answer to PR #32132. Summary:

After this PR is merged, **positive regulation of cytogamy (GO:7770079)** has the following parents:

- **Inferred `is_a`** (via ELK reasoning):
  - GO:0048522 `positive regulation of cellular process`
  - GO:2000243 `positive regulation of reproductive process`
- **Asserted `part_of`**: GO:0031139 `positive regulation of conjugation with cellular fusion`
- **Logical definition**: `biological regulation` **and** `positively_regulates` some `cytogamy`

The only parent removed by the PR is GO:7770078 (the grouping `regulation of cytogamy`), which is being obsoleted. No other parents are lost.
